description:
Ys VIII: Lacrimosa of Dana is an action RPG developed by Nihon Falcom. The player controls Adol Christin, a red-haired adventurer who finds himself stranded on the Isle of Seiren after his ship is attacked by monsters. 
The game features real-time combat, exploration, and a variety of characters to recruit and interact with. The main objective is to find a way off the island while uncovering its mysteries and forging bonds with the other survivors.

-Setup found here: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/junglechief87/Ys-8-AP-World/refs/heads/master/worlds/ys%208/Docs/Ys%208_en

v0.8.11.description:
Setup: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/junglechief87/Ys-8-AP-World/refs/heads/master/worlds/ys_8/Docs/Ys%208_en
- Fixed numerous issues with refactored rngPatcher file:
    - Fixed issue with certain chests not correctly populating events
    - Fixed issue with boss checkpoint event in village not correctly terminating

- Fixed issue with incorrect item IDs in client for Broken Super Weapons

*New Feature*
- Items in the Jewel Shop or hinted by NPCs will now be affixed with (!) when they're progression.
v0.8.11.title: v0.8.11

v0.8.2.description:
Setup: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/junglechief87/Ys-8-AP-World/refs/heads/master/worlds/ys_8/Docs/Ys%208_en

- Made handling of seed state in client better (Client Update)
- moved where the seed was set earlier in game scripts (Patcher Update)

Some late changes made before release to reduce false negatives were likely causing false positives on save files that shouldn't have passed the seed check. This simplifies the checks and makes it more reliable while reducing false positives by moving the seed much earlier in the game's initialization script and using a different flag in state checking.

v0.9.0.description:
Setup: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/junglechief87/Ys-8-AP-World/refs/heads/master/worlds/ys_8/Docs/Ys%208_en
### Bug Fixes
- Missing logic on chest in Path of the Insectoid Era added
- Fixed issue with reversed order of Progressive Shop Rank rewards in reward script.
### QoL
- All map icons for obstacles are placed on the map from game start
- Any items that set game flags should now have useful descriptions and are revealed in the collections menu on game start. I am hoping this will help new players know up front what all these items do and how to access each area of the world.
### New features
- Progressive Raid List is now an item in place of T's Notes. The first one adds Dogi to the village and the first 4 raids. There are 5 of them and raids and hunts are more staggered and intermixed. 
- A new option has been added called "Fun Items": these include new accessories that are only equip-able by Laxia, Sahad, Hummel, and Ricotta respectively. They are meant to be on Mistilteinn and Spirit Ring tiers of power and should make character choices more dynamic. There is also one new arm guard that is quite powerful and equip-able by everyone. 
 - Progressive Super Weapons option renamed to Progressive Super Items. If on with "Fun Items" it creates 4 new items that can be turned into Euron at max shop rank to get the ultimate accessories for Laxia, Sahad, Hummel, and Ricotta respectively.

v0.9.4.title: v0.9.4

v0.9.5.description:
### New Features
Lots of stuff has been done to help with new player on-boarding:
- Battle Logic now has a difficulty setting: 
  - Normal is essentially the same unchanged logic.
  - Hard puts fewer guarantees on shop ranks and party members while also reducing requirements for bosses (meaning it's less likely you'll be getting powerful equipment).
  - Easy pushes party members and shop ranks earlier, it guarantees access to super weapons at endgame and access to night raids earlier for ripe fruits and exp.
- A Helper Text toggle has been added. When activated this spawns additional text boxes in the game for local items and the client for remote items. This helps inform the players better on what items are and what they do. 
- Client now saves last used Slot and Host.
- New encounter added to the North Side of the island. The Primordial Den where Ricotta gets captured in the story previously did nothing. It is now a quick shot of experience for gaining access to the North Side. 
### Adjustments
- Master Kong used to logically require Mont Gendarme Night for Ripe Mango farming. This has been expanded to include Baja Tower and Nostalgia Cape (They both have a lone Ripe Mango Tree).
- Shuffled around how the Octus Region entrance was handled in the world to make its battle requirement work correctly.
- Death Link could sometimes cause soft locks if getting sent a death while in the warp menu. Warping now gets disabled until the player is killed to prevent this. 
- Death Link has also had parts of it's trigger adjusted such that it's more simultaneous across the party.
- Insect Repellent is now infinite use and getting Dina just gives one. 
- Selection Sphere enemies have been made more rewarding to defeat.
### Bug Fixes
- Fixed issue with Jade Pendant not setting flags for FSC Stairs when received remote.
- Village wasn't correctly setting flag for Hunt unlocks, this has been fixed.
- Mini bosses in Octus were supposed to be more rewarding in the rando. This was a bug that they weren't, it has been fixed and they are now an excellent experience source, about a Psyche's worth per.

*A few other minor code tweaks and adjustments that shouldn't be impactful*

#### *Make sure to patch files because base game scripts have been updated*

v1.0.0.description:
### New Features
- Boss Level Shuffle
    - Balanced: Boss levels are shuffled but distributed in a way to avoid extreme outliers and to keep early game bosses at relatively lower levels.
    - Chaotic: Boss levels are completely shuffled with no restrictions. This can lead to extreme outliers and early game bosses being late game levels.
- Dana bosses are now included in the Psyche pool. 
- Io is now a final boss option.
   - Past Dana damage types have been setup to correlate with other existing damage types. Slash -> Water, Strike -> Earth, Pierce -> Light
   - Logic accounts for damage types now.
       - Grazios  requires Slash
       - Nebritia requires Strike
       - Argura requires Slash and Strike
       - Crusos requires Pierce
       - Blasphima requires Slash and Strike
       - Le-Kyanos, Io, and Theos require Slash, Strike, and Pierce
- New entrances added to shuffle: Temple of the Great Tree Entrance and Temple of the Great Tree Garden Entrance
- Exp penalties for Raids and Hunts that existed in vanilla have been changed to an Exp bonus to make them more rewarding.
- Hunt bosses will spawn on the first wave for all Hunts, with the above bullet this should make them another good exp source.

### Logic Adjustments
- Gendarme Night chests: Mont Gendarme (Night) Cliffside Midpoint Chest, Mont Gendarme (Night) Mid-Boss Arena Chest, and Mont Gendarme (Night) Upper Cliffs Chest 1 now come into logic if the cabin can be reached during the day with access to the night hunt... of course.
- Purifying Bell chest is now logically accessible without being able to beat Doxa Griel. The event that triggers Doxa Griel now looks to see that the player has the Bell.

### Bug Fixes
- Client should no longer occasionally miss sending items sent by the server but not yet received by the current Ys 8 save file.
- More tweaks to make Octo Bosses behave better.
- Talk option removed from Thanatos outside Eternian Palace.

*I think that's everything?*

v1.1.0b.description:
### New Features
- Auto Tab compatibility with Pop Tracker
- Fast Intercepts Option
- Shuffle Damage Types Option
- Seiren Shuffle Lite now informs the user when there are script updates on the repo (there will be a "Phantom" notification for those that already have Seiren Shuffle Lite on their system even though no game script updates have been made since last version, this is because the field that is used to tag last update needs created on first patch)
### Options Adjustment
- Extra Flame Stones renamed to Extra Shop Ranks, clearer description of setting.
### Bug Fixes
- Fixed issue with boss stats variable persisting across different instances of Ys 8 in same seed
v1.1.0b.title: v1.1.0 beta

v1.1.1.description:
### Bug Fixes
- Flags around deathlink adjusted to increase stability (Saving now gets disabled when a deathlink is being received)
- Pirate Ship should no longer flood after completion
- Fixed logic typo for Mont Gendarme Night
- Added handling for if somehow the custom game over flag failed to unset, this seemed like a very rare thing that could happen.
v1.1.1.title: v1.1.1 beta

v1.2.0b.description:
### New Features
- Dana forms are now fully playable characters. They use Dana's weapons for their stats, they each only receive their 4 moves but still come with 2 to start. The characters when received are called Gratika and Luminous. They don't appear in the weapon shop menu due to bad interactions with weapon upgrades: Dana is temporarily put into the party if you have Gratika or Luminous, if she otherwise isn't, and Gratika and Luminous are temporarily removed when interacting with Kathleen. They do count towards castaway counts but the party is still capped at contributing a max of 6 to the castaway count. Party members over 6 get put into a reserved state, the player can interact with Paro in the village to adjust which characters are in the active party. 
- Some additional support has been added to hopefully allow better tracking of randomized entrances. 

### Notes
- Some buts are likely present, getting Luminous and Gratika to behave wasn't a clean integration. At this point my experience is this is playable and seems generally stable but I need a higher sample to find any issues that might still be lingering. So I'm releasing this as a beta.
v1.2.0b.title: 1.2.0 Beta
